level: Intermediate / AdvancedCollection: 2026Reference player: Paquito Navarro (Hack Series)Coach’s advice on the Bullpadel Hack 04 Hybrid CloudChoose this model if you want to relieve your arm or prevent injuries without switching to a purely defensive racket. Go for the Hybrid Cloud if you want that pleasant “trampoline” feel that helps launch the ball easily. It is the ideal tool for the all-around player who wants to keep pace in rallies while prioritizing comfort and forgiveness.

Intermediate / AdvancedCollection: 2026Reference player: Fede Chingotto (Neuron Series)Coach’s advice on the Bullpadel Neuron 02 CloudChoose this model if you are a right-side player or a strategist who likes to build the point rather than hit hard at all costs. Go for the Neuron Cloud if you have a history of tendinitis (tennis elbow), as its composition is one of the most arm-friendly on the market. It is the perfect choice to combine playing pleasure, physical safety, and technical progression.

level: Intermediate to AdvancedCollection: Bullpadel Women 2026Reference player: Delfi BreaCoach’s advice on the Bullpadel Vertex 05 Woman Cloud 2026Choose this racket if you are an intermediate to advanced player who favors an attacking game without wanting to suffer joint pain. Its soft core and vibration-absorbing materials are ideal for players looking for the perfect balance between hitting power and long-lasting comfort. Its rough finish adds valuable control on spin, a real asset for varying your game. On the other hand, beginners or players who prefer a defensive baseline game may be better suited to round shapes with a low balance. This Vertex 05 Woman Cloud remains a benchmark for players who want to combine dominance at the net with physical protection.
